title: centaurea jacea var. ruscinonensis
intro: Centaurea jacea var. ruscinonensis, also known as the brown knapweed or Russian knapweed, is a plant species within the family Asteraceae, characterized by its brown or reddish-brown flowers and a hairy stem. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea jacea var. ruscinonensis include its potential use in treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and combating bacterial infections. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions and wounds, or internally to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
